name: Avenc Tapat
equipment: 2 ropes (40 m and 25 m), several slings and carabiners
description: A distensional fracture in dolomitic breccias (NE-SW orientation) with a well-concealed small entrance (0.4 x 0.8 m). Despite the narrow fracture format, the walls are entirely covered in flowstone with beautiful curtain formations in overhanging areas and delicate speleothems affected by subsidence. The 56 m depth provides several hours of exploration through tight passages.
full_description:
Description

Located on the firebreak of the track that runs between the Coll des Pastors and the Mirador antennas, this distensional fracture develops in dolomitic breccias with a NE-SW orientation. The small entrance of 0.40 x 0.80 m was well covered, which made its discovery difficult.

After rigging on a pine tree found nearby, a descent of approximately 3.5 m takes us into the fracture proper, where the separation between the two sides of the fracture gives us an idea of what the rest of the visit will be like.

During the descent to the first level, we will be accompanied by some beautiful roots. Once we touch the ground, we will begin a descent, having to make a rebelay with a sling on a natural anchor through which the sling can be threaded (this is a good option if we don't want to place a spit, which would be the most appropriate thing to do). The descent to the next level, although very short (less than 3 m), is difficult. At this point, we must overcome a small escarpment in order to advance a few meters along the fracture or alternatively continue the descent by making another rebelay on a natural anchor. At the top of the "shaft," 24 m further down at -48 m, we reach the northern end of the last accessible level. Its length is 12 m, and at the southern end we can see that the fracture continues but is impassable.

Although this type of cavity does not lend itself to being particularly beautiful, it is not devoid of formations. In fact, its walls are entirely covered by flowstone, and in some cases with beautiful curtain formations in overhanging areas, not to mention certain corners where delicate speleothems grow, some of them affected by subsidence (collapse) processes.

Its 56 m of depth will allow us to spend several entertaining hours traversing a pothole where the narrow separation between the walls will at times put us to the test.
